channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.thesixthaxis.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.thesixthaxis.com

item.title: Nivalis renamed Nivalis Nights, release date set for September 29th
item.content:encoded:
If you enjoyed Cloudpunk then you may have been keeping an eye on Nivalis, the slice of life RPG set in the same world. Well, Nivalis no longer exists but it isn’t bad news. Instead, Nivalis has been rebranded as Nivalis Nights and it now has a release date. Developer ION Lands has confirmed that Nivalis Nights will launch on September 29th for Steam, and there is a new trailer introducing more of the world.

The trailer shows your humble beginnings of taking over a noodle stand, but life can take a lot of different turns. From this stand you can decide to build a thriving business that grows until it is the talk of the city, or spend your time fishing, decorating your apartment, or looking for other opportunities in the city. If life on the straight and narrow isn’t your vibe, you may want to extend your reach into the criminal underworld of the city, making money and a name for yourself there. ION Lands has stated that news and announcements about Nivalis Nights were quiet for a while because since October the studio was handling a legal matter that has since been resolved.

Cloudpunk Review

In our Cloudpunk review, Gareth said, “Cloudpunk delivers the look and feel of a cyberpunk city, it just doesn’t fill the city with anything of interest. The story is okay and there’s some side conversations to keep you amused, but once the novelty of driving around Nivalis wears off, you’ll recognise that this is a game made entirely of fetch quests. The city looks gorgeous, it’s just a shame it doesn’t have more attractions.”

Source: Steam



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/30/nivalis-renamed-nivalis-nights-release-date-set-for-september-29th/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Space Marine 2 dev shows off new Turok: Origins gameplay
item.content:encoded:
Saber Interactive, the developer behind acclaimed third person shooter Space Marine 2, has shared a new gameplay trailer for its upcoming Turok reboot.

Having tackled zombies in World War Z, and aliens in the Warhammer 40,000 universe, Saber are preparing to cross dinosaurs off their kill list when Turok: Origins launches later this year on PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S, PC, and Nintendo Switch 2.

The new trailer is brief, but gives us a closer look at some of the dino enemies players will come up against and the deadly finishers used to take them down. Turok: Origins is definitely channelling some of that Space Marine 2 energy here, seamlessly combining visceral melee and shooting action, featuring a cast of characters who each have their own unique loadouts.

While it can be played in solo it looks built for co-op fun, pitting squads of up to four dino hunters against waves of grunts and powerful bosses. Character progression is likely to play a big part here, with each hero having their own abilities to unlock and upgrade as well as scope for customisation.

There’s no launch date for Turok: Origins, though it is due to launch some time this autumn. Like many video game publishers, Saber Interactive has no doubt held back on committing to a release date until it gets a proper lay of the land in regards to the slew of heavy hitters already filling up the calendar.

For many gamers, this will likely be their first experience with the long-running series that first made its debut in 1997 for the Nintendo 64 (and PC). The FPS franchise doesn’t have quite the same cache as a DOOM or Quake with a previous attempt to reboot Turok in 2008 falling flat. However, there’s still some prestige surrounding the Turok name – this, combined with Saber’s recent track record, is more than enough to get us excited.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/06/30/space-marine-2-dev-shows-off-new-turok-origins-gameplay/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
